author: Henry Cooldown
title: GOAT
content: Best offline music app evr

author: ALP3R21
title: Random crashes on iOS 26.0.1
content: Fix it

author: ArchaiVR
title: No complaints
content: Good free player which had adds but never play audio or pause your music, importing local files can be a hassle, however importing through iTunes on a second device is easy. It’s good.

author: archae_ologist
title: Ads ads and more ads
content: Used to be good app and of course always have had ads but after recent updates you cannot do anything coz adds Have to wait & see 20 sec ads I chose this app due other apps had so many ads but seems time to find new one …

author: Barflairer
title: Intrusive pop up ads
content: Intrusive pop up ads within 5 seconds of opening the app for the first time that stops you from doing everything. If this is how they operate, I just know it’s going to get worse.

author: MacPro207
title: Please add tidal app support
content: This app would be so good if I could use tidal with this app

author: MattOz67
title: 6.2 broke adding iCloud files
content: 6.2 won’t allow me to add files as local files to a playlist.  The other method is not what I want to do

author: IIAmTheWalruss!
title: Perfect
content: Perfect for what I want it to be. Upload music with ease, layout is simple and makes sense. Tons of options. Constantly updated. Can't recommend enough - have been using for years.

author: Coop-a-doop
title: Too Many Issues for a Paid App
content: I have a large FLAC collection stored on my iPhone. The good is it plays FLAC. The bad is that I had to split my folders albums into multiple folders or simply wouldn’t add them to the library. I now have 7 sub folders on an iPhone containing various albums from 500 or so artists. The next issues is playlists, that entire function doesn’t work when dealing with local files. Whether creating a playlist and adding from the playlist tab, or browsing the music library and trying to add songs to an existing playlist, simply doesn’t work. After buying the premium version, I’m looking for something else. Disappointing.

author: hachieight
title: Tag gone missing after update
content: Was working fine before or in free version maybe? No songs or the albums photos displayed after paid for premium version or maybe cause by the latest update ?

author: not a gamer btw
title: Love it
content: Ty for frequent updates

author: fed up passanger
title: Update ruined what made it good
content: Now everything is organised just like iTunes which makes it one of the least user friendly experiences. If your albums aren’t properly labelled, they will get spit into a million folders and turn your library into a mess

author: bdboz
title: Great for offline music, bookmarks and variable speed pitch good for learning and practicing
content:
Use it offline music, bookmarks and variable speed pitch make it great for learning and practice. Much improvement over Musicbox which it resembles. 
Works well on an iPad in split screen mode, except for the “missing buttons” mentioned below.  The pitch/speed correction works well on an iPad, at least the high end one I use it on. 
==Developer- really would be useful to have a pause/play button in bookmark mode, and a rewind 10 secs button. :)

author: Doesn’t work - not happy
title: Doesn’t work
content: Totally agree with the last reviewer. It’s all over the place. It forgets your playlists and can’t find album artwork or organise your local files by filename. Really not happy I spent $15 on this.

author: Doombringer12
title: All over the place
content: Spent the money on the premium and it’s all over the place. Downloaded songs go missing or corrupt all the time. One day the full album is there the next day songs are corrupt and no longer can be played or edited. Stopped using Vox to try this app out as I liked the interface with OneDrive but regret the 15$ spent. Basically unusable.

author: dutcvvg
title: n
content: import apple music

author: NameisNemo
title: Worse app for music
content: If you are an audiophile stay away. Never above 96khz, no matter what.

author: AA_ZZe
title: Would be 5 stars but inconsistent recognition of tags for offline media
content:
For whatever reason doesn’t list all ‘albums’ by an artist even when the songs from these albums appear in the list when you toggle between songs/albums view after having located an artist.

All tags are identical, I have even deleted the entire media library and rebuilt but the behaviour persists.  Makes no sense at all.

author: wanderm
title: Plucky independent
content:
Flacbox delivers with being both a streamer and playing with your (lossless these days!) pay-to-“own” files.  The full version isn’t cheap, but there is quality in the finer details.

I don’t have a problem with Apple Music / iTunes, and I certainly don’t resent my iDevice, but as an old-timer I really appreciate this “old-school” substitute.

author: Jim Tao 888
title: Interface not too friendly
content: But qulity is good

author: Naezeroth
title: App used to work
content: It used to work very well, but since a few updates ago when I use the app when my phone is locked or on a different app whilst playing music it crashes - this is all on the premium version of the app too!

author: Shelley6148
title: Tinder for audio
content: All promises - no delivery unless paid for first. Getting tired of this method of ‘trial’.

author: MrDanteDavid
title: New update makes mp3 unplayable
content: Love this app and all its features but something in the new update causes mp3 files to not be playable. It shows up fine in the library but when you tap on them, the audio does not start.

author: A245245542643263
title: No m4b chapter support and no sound on iPad
content: No m4b chapter support, and no sound on iPad, although there is sound on iPhone.

author: Huong So
title: Sound bad
content: Sound is inrerreupred

author: @Abi1
title: Abi
content: Change the app icon then i will rate 5 star

author: Pauli-au
title: So much potential but far too many bugs
content: I’ve been using this for almost 12 months now.   I really wanted to buy the Pro version but can’t bring myself as It has too many bugs when connecting to network music sources.  Also various other bugs that pop up.  So much potential, but in the end just frustration.

author: Jing Vin
title: 5 stars music app
content: Premium build. Soo happly with this app! Thanks the Developers!!!! Good job! ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

author: BigNat74
title: Awesome App
content: Does everything I need in a flac music player

author: David JH
title: Won’t even load on iOS 12.3.1
content: This app explodes at the start screen. Don’t bother

author: Rawlo WA
title: Valuable app
content: Easy to use, clear interface, works with FLAC files on a NAS and Chromecast Audio

content: Need ape support!!!

content: Great app, does exactly as stated!

author: Toodamnloud
title: Great player.
content: Cloud access is flawless, but as already stated, why are songs not in running order, but alphabetical?

author: The original Blurk
title: Missing a critical feature
content: This is a perfectly decent media player app but for me it's missing a critical feature so I can only give it a middling score. It doesn't appear to support gapless playback which, in my opinion, should be an essential feature for anyone interested in the full album or live recording experience.

author: Sakii_kn
title: It's great
content: This app is probably the best one the I've used so far
